Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505 - Matter Not Reported

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank appeared represented by Jonathan Vaknin in court.
Defendant Sergio Suarez not appearing.

The court rules on this matter based on the pleadings and the evidence submitted.

Damages: $27,348.90
Prejudgment Interest: $0
Attorney Fees: $0
Costs: $370.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $27,718.90

Proposed judgment signed and filed this date.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Continued To Dec-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Sergio Suarez

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: D. Carter; - Matter not reported

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank appeared in court represented by Roger Jackson.
No appearance by Defendant Sergio Suarez.

This matter is continued to December 12, 2025 at 9:00am in dept. 505 as the Plaintiff does not have a proposed judgment prepared for the court's review and signature.

Counsel was instructed to prepare and submit one ahead of the next calling of this matter.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted
Off Calendar Oct-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Wells Fargo bank, N.A.'s Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted is GRANTED.

Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(c), upon motion the court shall deem unanswered requests for admissions (RFAs) admitted, unless it finds the responding party, before hearing on the motion, served a proposed response to the requests for admission that is in substantial compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.220.

The record here demonstrates Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. sent requests for admissions that appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Defendant Sergios A Suarez ultimately failed to answer/respond. The record does not demonstrate her failure was reasonable or justified. The requests set out in Plaintiff's Request for Admissions, Set One, are deemed admitted and the genuineness of the relevant documents is established.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted
